AI Carbon Monitor

AI Carbon Tracking
and Optimization

Track, analyze, and optimize the energy consumption of your machine learning models with our advanced monitoring solution.

🌱

Eco-Friendly AI

Visualize exact CO2 emissions generated by your training and inference workloads.

⚡

Real-Time Stats

Live updates on CPU, GPU, and Memory power consumption with minimal overhead.

💡

Smart Suggestions

Get actionable insights on how to reduce your carbon footprint and optimize efficiency.

🧠 Model List (Best for Your Project)

Select a workload to understand its footprint. This covers ML + DL + NLP + CV.

🧩 System Connection Flow

1 Model Script
↓
2 CarbonTracker.start()
↓
3 Model Runs / Training
↓
4 CarbonTracker.stop()
↓
5 CO₂ Stored in DB
↓
6 Dashboard + Suggestions

🧠 Model Comparison Table

Model Type Purpose Why Good?
Dummy Workload CPU Baseline Easy, low CO₂
LSTM ML / DL Time-series / text Medium load
ResNet-18 CNN Image classification Real DL example
BERT (Mini) Transformer NLP High CO₂ demo
YOLOv5-Nano Object Detection Real-time inference Continuous CO₂

🧮 Proper Calculation Metrics

Standardized emission tracking using physical laws and carbon intensity.

Energy (kWh) = (Power[W] × Time[h]) / 1000

1. Energy Integration

We sample power consumption (Watts) every second and integrate it over the workload duration to get total kWh.

Mass (CO₂e) = kWh × Carbon Intensity

2. Carbon Mapping

Total energy is multiplied by the regional grid's intensity factor (e.g., 0.475 kg/kWh) to get the CO₂ equivalent.

mg CO₂ = (kg CO₂) × 1,000,000

3. Unit Conversion

AI workloads often produce small emissions. We convert kg to milligrams (mg) for high-precision real-time display.

System Requirements

Core Software

  • Python 3.9+
  • Node.js 16+
  • Git Latest

AI Frameworks

  • PyTorch 2.0+
  • CodeCarbon 2.3+
  • FastAPI 0.10+
© 2024 AI Carbon Tracking and Optimization. Built for a sustainable future.
); }; export default LandingPage;
